handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:189:- New `parse_swarm_condition_n` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` discriminates `n<digits>` from `oneshot` / `hybrid_v1` / malformed. PREREG_AMENDMENT § 3 condition 2 cleared.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:212:- New module `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs`. Pure stdlib (zero new deps). 7-variant `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31). `FC_TRACE=1` gate cached in `OnceLock`; `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:213:- 9 wired anchor sites total (round-1 had 6 — A8e fix F4 added 3 swarm verify sites): synthetic short-circuit / mr tick / OMEGA full-proof / OMEGA per-tactic / natural MaxTxExhausted (with budget_regime payload from A5) / oneshot FC1-N12 verify bracket / **swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (alone + tape+payload retry)** / **swarm `verify_partial`**.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:220:- `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change: per-provider multi-key round-robin. PROVIDERS map now holds a list of env names per provider; `get_client_round_robin` distributes via `_rr_counters` mod `len(clients)`. `/stats` exposes `per_key_requests` for observability. New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:245:(Round-1 risk #5 "No FC1-N12 emit in run_swarm verify path" was CLOSED by A8e fix F4 — both auditors flagged the staleness in round-2 (Codex R2#3, Gemini R2#2). Removed from this list.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:250:- (Q1.a) Each of A1–A7 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the commit message, per memory `feedback_fc_first_problem_handling`. Verify by re-reading each commit message. Are any commits missing FC-trace, or claiming an FC node that doesn't match the actual code change?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:338:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:392:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:402:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5102:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5105:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit (swarm), FC2-N20 mr tick (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic (swarm), FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (swarm, with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (verify_omega_detailed × 2 + verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5106:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5107:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5108:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5109:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5110:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5604:   144	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5640:   180	- `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change: per-provider multi-key round-robin. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5670:   210	- (Q1.a) Each of A1–A7 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the commit message, per memory `feedback_fc_first_problem_handling`. Verify by re-reading each commit message. Are any commits missing FC-trace, or claiming an FC node that doesn't match the actual code change?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5751:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5755:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5809:    85	"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5819:    95	- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6749:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6750:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6751:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6752:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6754:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6756:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6760:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:42:- Codex R2: **CHALLENGE / high** — 3 findings (F2 not recurring; PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 still says "strictest plausible bar" contradicting § 2; A8 packet + TRACE_MATRIX stale on counts + risk #5 + run_corr_id symbol row + "in CI" claim).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6761:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:49:- **G2** (Codex R2#2): `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded to remove the residual "strictest plausible bar is conservative" phrase that contradicted § 2's wording correction. Re-hashed in Trust Root.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6762:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:55:- (RQ7) Verify G2: re-grep `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` for any remaining "strictest" / "conservative" claim; the only acceptable use is the round-1 wording-correction note in § 2.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6766:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:80:- (RQ10) Verify H1: re-grep `A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md` for any remaining "conservative" / "strictest" claim about `p_0`. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6767:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:83:- (RQ13) Verify packet self-consistency: any other "conservative" claims about the substitution? Any other anchor-count mismatches? Any other contradictions between round-1 questions and round-2/3 closures?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6768:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:144:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6831:handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6851:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit (swarm), FC2-N20 mr tick (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic (swarm), FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (swarm, with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (verify_omega_detailed × 2 + verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:7890:     8	- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:7893:    11	- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit (swarm), FC2-N20 mr tick (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic (swarm), FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (swarm, with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (verify_omega_detailed × 2 + verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:7894:    12	- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:7895:    13	- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:7898:    16	- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8142:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8143:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8144:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8145:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8146:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8151:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:42:- Codex R2: **CHALLENGE / high** — 3 findings (F2 not recurring; PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 still says "strictest plausible bar" contradicting § 2; A8 packet + TRACE_MATRIX stale on counts + risk #5 + run_corr_id symbol row + "in CI" claim).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8152:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:48:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8153:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:49:- **G2** (Codex R2#2): `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded to remove the residual "strictest plausible bar is conservative" phrase that contradicted § 2's wording correction. Re-hashed in Trust Root.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8154:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:50:- **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2): A8 packet § 2 cumulative-test table extended with A8e + A8e2 columns; § 4 Trust-Root-clean assertion bumped 30→34; § 5 Risk #5 removed (closed by F4); § 6 Q7.a 261→265 + Q7.b 30→34. TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2".

handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:3:**Round**: R2
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5:**Test baseline**: 264 PASS + 29 ignored + 0 failed (Rust); 15/15 PASS (Python proxy tests)
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:18:I have reviewed the A8 packet, the six fixes (F1–F6), and the associated source code. The fixes substantively address the letter of the five findings from Codex and the VETO-level finding from Gemini in the round-1 audit. The unification of `run_id` (F1), the correction of the routing matrix (F3), and the improved wording of the PREREG amendment (F6) are particularly well-executed.
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:24:The round-1 VETO was predicated on the absence of an automated test to pin the `llm_proxy.py` round-robin invariant, a critical mitigation for the V3L-27 collapse. Fix F2 (`scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`) provides the necessary test artifact, and its inclusion in the Trust Root (per `genesis_payload.toml`) prevents its silent modification.
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:54:The fixes are substantial and address the core technical issues from the round-1 audit. The remaining defects are procedural and documentary, not architectural. They can and must be corrected in-cycle before Phase B is authorized. The primary fix (Finding 1) is to provide evidence that the new conformance test is actually part of an automated quality gate, fully closing the loop on the round-1 VETO.

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3746:./handover/audits/SYNTHESIS_2026-04-22.md:79:| `bus.rs:416-424` `Box<dyn>` downcast ~5× 加速 | 不是 per-tx，仅 bus-side invest/refund/settle 路径 | ~1.05-1.1× 加速 | **Claude 严重失真**（5× vs 1.1×） |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3759:./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_2026-04-21.md:83:| `bus.rs:416-424` `Box<dyn>` downcast | ~5× 加速 | **~1.05× - 1.1×** 加速 | Claude 的估计严重失真。`downcast` 本质上是一次 vtable 查找加一次类型 ID 比较，耗时仅为几个 CPU cycle。在只有 3-5 个 tool 的情况下，`enum` dispatch（通常是 jump table）相比动态分派的优势微乎其微。5倍的加速意味着 downcast 占了函数 80% 的执行时间，这在物理上是不可能的。实际加速效果几乎无法测量。 |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3832:./handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:78:### 2. `src/bus.rs:416-424` `Box<dyn TuringTool>` + downcast claim
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3833:./handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:80:Again, the performance concern is overstated as phrased. `debit_wallet` and `credit_wallet` do perform repeated `manifest()` + `downcast_mut::<WalletTool>()` scans (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:416-437`), but those functions are not on every transaction. They are used on bus-side invest/refund/hayek/settlement paths (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:233,240,346,359,383-412`), while evaluator-side invest does its own separate wallet downcasts (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:694-716`). The deeper issue is architectural duplication and bus bypass, not the raw dynamic-dispatch cost alone.

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4391:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:10:| FC1-N1 | `Q_t = ⟨q_t, HEAD_t, tape_t⟩` | `TuringBus::q_state`<br>`Tape::time_arrow`<br>`Kernel::tape` | `src/bus.rs:70`<br>`src/ledger.rs:146`<br>`src/kernel.rs:20` | `Y`<br>`N`<br>`N` | `medium` | Runtime `Q_t` is distributed across bus state, the tape tail used as HEAD, and the kernel-held tape rather than a single `Q_t` struct. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4392:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:11:| FC1-N2 | `q_t` | `QState`<br>`TuringBus::q_state` | `src/bus.rs:53`<br>`src/bus.rs:70` | `Y`<br>`Y` | `high` | Phase 8.E makes `q_t` explicit as `QState::{Running,Halted{reason}}` stored on the bus. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4393:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:13:| FC1-N4 | `tape_t` | `Tape`<br>`Kernel::tape`<br>`UniverseSnapshot::tape` | `src/ledger.rs:44`<br>`src/kernel.rs:20`<br>`src/sdk/snapshot.rs:23` | `Y`<br>`N`<br>`N` | `high` | The append-only DAG tape is the concrete world-state carrier in kernel storage and agent snapshots. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4394:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:14:| FC1-N5 | `rtool` | `ReadTool::project`<br>`DefaultReadTool::project`<br>`TuringBus::snapshot` | `src/sdk/read_tool.rs:24`<br>`src/sdk/read_tool.rs:37`<br>`src/bus.rs:802` | `Y`<br>`N`<br>`Y` | `medium` | Phase Z added an explicit `rtool` trait, but the current evaluator still reads state by calling `bus.snapshot()` directly. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4395:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:15:| FC1-N6 | `input = ⟨q_i, s_i⟩` | `UniverseSnapshot`<br>`build_agent_prompt`<br>`let prompt = build_agent_prompt(...)`<br>`GenerateRequest` | `src/sdk/snapshot.rs:22`<br>`src/sdk/prompt.rs:15`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:619`<br>`src/drivers/llm_http.rs:14` | `Y`<br>`Y`<br>`N`<br>`Y` | `high` | What the agent sees is the frozen snapshot rendered into a prompt and shipped as the LLM request message. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4396:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:16:| FC1-N7 | `δ / AI` | `ResilientLLMClient::generate`<br>`match client.generate(&request).await` | `src/drivers/llm_http.rs:84`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:211`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:644` | `Y`<br>`N`<br>`N` | `high` | These are the actual LLM call surfaces that map prompt input to model output in oneshot and swarm runs. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4397:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:20:| FC1-N11 | `∏p predicates` | `TuringBus::evaluate_predicates`<br>`Predicate` | `src/bus.rs:148`<br>`src/sdk/predicate.rs:88` | `Y`<br>`Y` | `medium` | The explicit product-evaluation API exists, but current runtime gating is still split across this Phase Z surface and legacy bus/oracle checks. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4398:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:22:| FC1-N13 | `wtool` | `WriteTool::write`<br>`DefaultWriteTool::write`<br>`TuringBus::append`<br>`TuringBus::append_oracle_accepted` | `src/sdk/write_tool.rs:29`<br>`src/sdk/write_tool.rs:84`<br>`src/bus.rs:324`<br>`src/bus.rs:347` | `Y`<br>`N`<br>`Y`<br>`Y` | `medium` | The explicit write-tool contract exists, but the current harness usually enters the write path by calling bus append methods directly. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4399:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:23:| FC1-N14 | `Q_{t+1}` on successful write | `TuringBus::append_internal`<br>`Kernel::append`<br>`TuringBus::halt_with_reason` | `src/bus.rs:421`<br>`src/kernel.rs:108`<br>`src/bus.rs:207` | `N`<br>`Y`<br>`Y` | `medium` | Successful writes update tape and ledger in `append_internal`, and terminal writes additionally advance `q_state` through `halt_with_reason`. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4400:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:24:| FC1-N15 | `Q_t` branch on `∏p = 0` | `PartialVerdict::Reject`<br>`BusResult::Vetoed` | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/lean4_oracle.rs:328`<br>`src/bus.rs:111` | `Y`<br>`N` | `high` | Rejected steps and vetoed appends explicitly encode the no-write branch where state is preserved. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4402:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:29:| FC2-N20 | `initAI --once→ mr` | `let tick_interval: usize`<br>`TuringBus::emit_mr_tick_node` | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:459`<br>`src/bus.rs:385` | `N`<br>`Y` | `high` | Swarm initialization configures the tick cadence once, and the later tick path reduces through `emit_mr_tick_node`. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4403:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:30:| FC2-N21 | `initAI --once→ Q0` | `Kernel::new`<br>`TuringBus::new`<br>`TuringBus::init` | `src/kernel.rs:50`<br>`src/bus.rs:115`<br>`src/bus.rs:299` | `N`<br>`N`<br>`Y` | `high` | Kernel construction, bus construction, and `init()` collectively materialize the initial live machine state before iteration begins. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4404:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:31:| FC2-N22 | `HALT` | `QState::Halted`<br>`TuringBus::halt_with_reason`<br>`TuringBus::halt_and_settle` | `src/bus.rs:55`<br>`src/bus.rs:207`<br>`src/bus.rs:581` | `N`<br>`Y`<br>`Y` | `high` | The terminal node is an explicit halted state plus the halt path that emits durable halt events and final settlement. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4405:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:33:| FC2-N24 | `clock` | `TuringBus::clock`<br>`for tx in 0..max_transactions`<br>`let tick_interval: usize` | `src/bus.rs:66`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:485`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:459` | `N`<br>`N`<br>`N` | `high` | Scheduling comes from the transaction loop and tick interval, while the bus tracks a monotone committed clock counter. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4406:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:34:| FC2-N25 | `mr` | `let mr_summary = format!(...)`<br>`TuringBus::emit_mr_tick_node` | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:504`<br>`src/bus.rs:385` | `N`<br>`Y` | `medium` | Map-reduce is implemented inline in the swarm tick block rather than as a dedicated `mr` function. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4408:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:37:| FC2-N28 | `tools_other` | `WriteTool::write_with_tools`<br>`TuringBus::tools`<br>`bus.mount_tool(...)` | `src/sdk/write_tool.rs:57`<br>`src/bus.rs:64`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:407`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:414`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:415`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:419` | `Y`<br>`N`<br>`N`<br>`N`<br>`N`<br>`N` | `high` | Ancillary tools are carried as mounted `TuringTool`s on the bus, and `write_with_tools` names that exact contract explicitly. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4409:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:38:| FC3-N29 | `boot` | `async fn main`<br>`TuringBus::boot` | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:88`<br>`src/bus.rs:286` | `N`<br>`Y` | `medium` | Process entry is `main`, and the bus exposes a boot hook for mounted tools even though the current harness goes straight to `init()`. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4410:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:40:| FC3-N31 | `logs archive` | `TuringBus::with_wal_path`<br>`Wal::replay`<br>`Wal::write_event` | `src/bus.rs:227`<br>`src/wal.rs:70`<br>`src/wal.rs:54` | `Y`<br>`Y`<br>`N` | `high` | Persisted history is the WAL-backed archive that replays old state and appends new ledger events durably. |

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4412:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:44:| FC3-N35 | `anti-oreo: top → agents → tools` | `TuringBus::evaluate_predicates`<br>`let agent_ids`<br>`TuringTool` | `src/bus.rs:148`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:431`<br>`src/sdk/tool.rs:38` | `Y`<br>`N`<br>`Y` | `medium` | The chain exists as predicate gating over a swarm of agent ids acting through mounted tools, but there is no single anti-oreo coordinator type. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4413:./handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:46:| FC3-N37 | `tools` | `TuringTool`<br>`WalletTool`<br>`SearchTool`<br>`LibrarianTool`<br>`Lean4Oracle` | `src/sdk/tool.rs:38`<br>`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs:24`<br>`src/sdk/tools/search.rs:11`<br>`src/sdk/tools/librarian.rs:17`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/lean4_oracle.rs:42` | `Y`<br>`Y`<br>`Y`<br>`Y`<br>`N` | `high` | The tool layer is the `TuringTool` trait plus the mounted concrete implementations the swarm can use. |

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4569:experiments/minif2f_v4/src/jsonl_schema.rs:8:// era jsonl rows (the `PputResult` shape emitted by evaluator before this commit)
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4640:handover/audits/run_codex_pput_ccl_b2_b4_audit.sh:83:- (Q5.c) RunAggregate has `progress: u8` (Lean ground truth). PputResult does NOT have `progress` — only `verified: Option<bool>`. Is this gap intentional (B5 will fill it) or a bug?
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4642:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:21:2.  **Schema Drift from B1 Contract (Q5.c)**: The B1 `RunAggregate` v2 schema mandates a `progress: u8` field. The B4 implementation in `PputResult` emits `verified: Option<bool>` (`evaluator.rs:90`). While semantically similar, this is a direct violation of the frozen schema's type and name contract. Downstream tooling built against the B1 spec will break or require shims.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4645:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:64:*   **(Q4.a) Round-trip preserved**: **PASS**. All new fields in `PputResult` are `Option<T>` and use `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` (`evaluator.rs:82-93`). This is the canonical and correct way to ensure that deserializing old data and re-serializing it produces identical output.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4649:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:74:*   **(Q5.c) `progress: u8` vs `verified: Option<bool>`**: **CHALLENGE (Major Bug)**. This is a clear deviation. The B1 `RunAggregate` schema specifies `progress: u8`. The B4 implementation adds `verified: Option<bool>` (`evaluator.rs:90`). The emitted JSONL will have a field named `verified` with a boolean value, not a field named `progress` with a numeric value. This breaks the B1 contract and will fail validation against a strict schema-checker.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4657:handover/audits/CONSTITUTIONAL_BLINDSPOT_AUDIT_2026-04-22.md:40:Ruling: "被他人调用次数"是 Art. I.2 明文要求的三大统计信号之一，必须作为 per-agent 累积标量暴露给 top management + agent snapshot。实现：`Tape` 级增加 `reputation_by_author: HashMap<String, u32>`，每次 `append` 把所有 `citations` 的 author 的 count +1；进入 `UniverseSnapshot`；PputResult 增加 `reputation_at_end` 字段。

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5275:handover/ai-direct/EXT_AUDIT_PHASE_8_R1ALPHA/brief.md:35:Removed silent in-memory fallback in run_oneshot. If `TuringBus::with_wal_path` fails, emit `MEASUREMENT_ERROR oneshot WAL: <e>` and return a non-result PputResult (condition=`measurement_error`, solved=false, tx=1). Batch runner retries; no bus without WAL will ever claim OMEGA.

If round-1 Phase E reveals the auto-loop is broken (e.g., 0 of k artifacts pass any test), the arc is declared a NEGATIVE finding. handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:6037:handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:80:Again, the performance concern is overstated as phrased. `debit_wallet` and `credit_wallet` do perform repeated `manifest()` + `downcast_mut::<WalletTool>()` scans (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:416-437`), but those functions are not on every transaction. They are used on bus-side invest/refund/hayek/settlement paths (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:233,240,346,359,383-412`), while evaluator-side invest does its own separate wallet downcasts (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:694-716`). The deeper issue is architectural duplication and bus bypass, not the raw dynamic-dispatch cost alone.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:6109:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3833:./handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:80:Again, the performance concern is overstated as phrased. `debit_wallet` and `credit_wallet` do perform repeated `manifest()` + `downcast_mut::<WalletTool>()` scans (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:416-437`), but those functions are not on every transaction. They are used on bus-side invest/refund/hayek/settlement paths (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:233,240,346,359,383-412`), while evaluator-side invest does its own separate wallet downcasts (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:694-716`). The deeper issue is architectural duplication and bus bypass, not the raw dynamic-dispatch cost alone.

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:6329:Q4/Q5: Backward read compatibility is okay for old rows through `LegacyRunAggregate` flattening, but new rows are also legacy-classified because evaluator emits no `schema_version`. Field spellings present in `PputResult` match v2 names, but the set misses `progress`, `rollback_count`, guardrails, model snapshot, mode, etc. `verified: Option<bool>` is not a substitute for `progress: u8`.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:475:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:479:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:480:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:533:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:543:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5399:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5401:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5402:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5403:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (3 entries: `llm_proxy.py` + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5404:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5406:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5407:- A8e5 (post-A8e4, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-5 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — same split as round-4): five narrow doc/source-text fixes; zero source-code changes. **J1** Round-4 outcome + A8e4 fixes shipped section added to the packet (was missing — Codex R5#1 caught the gap). **J2** `<pending>` commit placeholders for A8e2/A8e3/A8e4 replaced with actual SHAs (`0af47b7` / `3d38ba5` / `8693789`); A8e5 self-reference now uses "this commit" to break the placeholder-staleness pattern. **J3** packet § 6 Q6 question text reworded: "24 → 30 / 6 new entries" → "24 → 34 / 10 new entries"; full enumerated list added. **J4** TRACE_MATRIX § 1 top-bullet TR-deltas corrected: A5 `25 → 26` → `26 → 27`; A6 `26 → 27` → `27 → 28` (matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header). **J5** round-3 retrospective text about `make_pput` arg count: 21 → 24 (Gemini R5 spot-check; F1's `run_id` parameter brought the post-A8e count from 21 to 24). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (no new files; doc-only changes). All findings are documentary; both auditors confirmed the code is sound + ready for Phase B.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5408:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5409:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5410:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5411:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5452:| `parse_swarm_condition_n` (A2) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | FC2-N16 InitAI orchestration entry — discriminates `oneshot` vs `n<N>` swarm code paths; FC1-N11 ∏p reached only via swarm | ✅ |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5460:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |
