handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5713:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5714:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5715:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:12:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (3 entries: `llm_proxy.py` + 2 smoke scripts).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5723:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:20:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5727:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:69:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5734:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:113:- 🔨/⚠️ count: 0 → **0** (no actionable rows pending in v2 scope)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5735:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:120:- B7-extra round-1 audit-fix → 20
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5741:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5742:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5752:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:16:- Trust Root: 30-entry manifest (round-1 packet's count; later corrected to 31 — see round-2)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5758:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:53:- Merged: **CHALLENGE**. No VETO. Both auditors confirmed round-1 fixes are letter-correct; remaining gaps procedural/documentary.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5759:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:56:1. (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) F2's `test_llm_proxy.py` exists but is documented as manual-only; no CI integration. Tests-not-running-automatically = documentation, not gates. Closes Gemini's round-1 VETO at the *artifact* level but NOT at the *process* level.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5814:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:135:(All round-1..round-7 questions and their closures are recorded in `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md`. The questions below are for the current-round reviewer pass.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5827:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5829:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:31:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5830:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5835:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5845:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:57:2. (Codex R2#2) PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 was corrected (F6) but § 8 audit-requirements paragraph still says "strictest plausible bar is conservative" — direct contradiction with § 2.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:9638:   `PREREG_AMENDMENT` § 2 correctly says § 3 conditions are prerequisites and “do not by themselves guarantee” calibration before any phase, and that Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution if calibration has not completed: [PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:31](</home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:31>), [PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32](</home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:32>). But § 8 still says the substitution is accepted because “§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first”: [PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95](</home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95>). That directly fails packet Q2.a/Q2.b: [A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:143](</home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:143>), [A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:144](</home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:144>). This is fixable wording, but it is in a Trust Root contract, so I cannot PASS.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:9641:   The history claims M4 reconciled § 2/§ 3/§ 8: [A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:204](</home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:204>), but finding 1 shows § 8 still contains the stale false claim. The same section still has an unresolved placeholder for the A8e8 commit: [A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:200](</home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:200>), and the cumulative table still marks round 7 pending despite the round-7 verdicts being recorded above: [A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:184](</home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:184>), [A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:219](</home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:219>). This undermines Q5.b’s append-only chronology auditability.

handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_R1R8_2026-04-22.md:12:### R2 — VETO cleared?
handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_R1R8_2026-04-22.md:37:All VETO and CHALLENGE items from the prior dual audit have been comprehensively and correctly addressed. The two critical VETOs on receipt forgeability (R1) and oneshot durability (R2) are fully resolved with robust fixes. All challenges are cleared with correct logic, extensive new tests, and improved documentation. The amendment is of high quality and can proceed to Phase 2 A/B testing.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5:**Test baseline**: 264 PASS + 29 ignored + 0 failed (Rust); 15/15 PASS (Python proxy tests)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:51:**Document split (A8e7)**: this packet is the **stable current-state snapshot** of Phase A at exit. The chronological history of audit rounds 1–N + their in-cycle fix bundles lives in the **companion** document `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` (append-only). Past audit transcripts are at `handover/audits/{CODEX,GEMINI}_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26{,_R2,_R3,_R4,_R5,_R6,...}.md`. Reviewers needing the full chronology read the history doc; reviewers verifying current-state correctness against Phase B prerequisites read THIS packet.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:107:- **Statistical framing** (post-A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 wording corrections): `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows; `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:111:- New `parse_swarm_condition_n` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` discriminates `n<digits>` from `oneshot` / `hybrid_v1` / malformed. PREREG_AMENDMENT § 3 condition 2 cleared.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:133:- New `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs`. Pure stdlib (zero new deps). 7-variant `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31). `FC_TRACE=1` gate cached in `OnceLock`; `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:178:(All round-1..round-6 questions and their closures are recorded in `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md`. The questions below are the round-N+1 set — a fresh reviewer pass that does NOT need to re-litigate prior closures.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:191:- (Q3.a) Each of A1–A7 + A8e..A8e6 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the message. Verify by re-reading commit messages. Any commit missing FC-trace? Any commit that touched `constitution.md`?

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:276:1. (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) F2's `test_llm_proxy.py` exists but is documented as manual-only; no CI integration. Tests-not-running-automatically = documentation, not gates. Closes Gemini's round-1 VETO at the *artifact* level but NOT at the *process* level.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:397:**Trigger**: rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern that single-point fixes could not close. **Diagnosis**: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology in one document. **Fix** (commit `<this commit's SHA>` — A8e7): split into two docs following the project's existing constitutional pattern (stable spec + delta log; cf. constitution.md + Art. V.3 amendment log; PREREG + PREREG_AMENDMENT; TRACE_MATRIX_v0 + v1 + v2). The packet becomes current-state only; THIS history doc becomes append-only chronology.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:452:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:456:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:510:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:520:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5267:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5269:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5270:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5271:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (3 entries: `llm_proxy.py` + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5273:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5274:- A8e5 (post-A8e4, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-5 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — same split as round-4): five narrow doc/source-text fixes; zero source-code changes. **J1** Round-4 outcome + A8e4 fixes shipped section added to the packet (was missing — Codex R5#1 caught the gap). **J2** `<pending>` commit placeholders for A8e2/A8e3/A8e4 replaced with actual SHAs (`0af47b7` / `3d38ba5` / `8693789`); A8e5 self-reference now uses "this commit" to break the placeholder-staleness pattern. **J3** packet § 6 Q6 question text reworded: "24 → 30 / 6 new entries" → "24 → 34 / 10 new entries"; full enumerated list added. **J4** TRACE_MATRIX § 1 top-bullet TR-deltas corrected: A5 `25 → 26` → `26 → 27`; A6 `26 → 27` → `27 → 28` (matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header). **J5** round-3 retrospective text about `make_pput` arg count: 21 → 24 (Gemini R5 spot-check; F1's `run_id` parameter brought the post-A8e count from 21 to 24). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (no new files; doc-only changes). All findings are documentary; both auditors confirmed the code is sound + ready for Phase B.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5275:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5276:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5277:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5278:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5319:| `parse_swarm_condition_n` (A2) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | FC2-N16 InitAI orchestration entry — discriminates `oneshot` vs `n<N>` swarm code paths; FC1-N11 ∏p reached only via swarm | ✅ |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5327:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |

The chronological history of audit rounds 1–N + their in-cycle fix bundles lives in the **companion** document `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` (append-only). Past audit transcripts are at `handover/audits/{CODEX,GEMINI}_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26{,_R2,_R3,_R4,_R5,_R6,...}.md`. Reviewers needing the full chronology read the history doc; reviewers verifying current-state correctness against Phase B prerequisites read THIS packet.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5642:    67	- **Statistical framing** (post-A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 wording corrections): `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows; `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5930:   177	**Trigger**: rounds 2–6 produced a recurring documentary CHALLENGE pattern that single-point fixes could not close. **Diagnosis**: `A8_EXIT_PACKET` was conflating stable-snapshot artifact + append-only chronology in one document. **Fix** (commit `<this commit's SHA>` — A8e7): split into two docs following the project's existing constitutional pattern (stable spec + delta log; cf. constitution.md + Art. V.3 amendment log; PREREG + PREREG_AMENDMENT; TRACE_MATRIX_v0 + v1 + v2). The packet becomes current-state only; THIS history doc becomes append-only chronology.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6168:    84	    let keys: Vec<&str> = entries.iter().map(|(k, _)| k.as_str()).collect();
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6295:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6299:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6353:    85	"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6363:    95	- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6378:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6379:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6380:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:18:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6381:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:19:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6383:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:67:- **Statistical framing** (post-A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 wording corrections): `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows; `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6385:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:9983:11:**Document split (A8e7)**: this packet is the **stable current-state snapshot** of Phase A at exit. The chronological history of audit rounds 1–N + their in-cycle fix bundles lives in the **companion** document `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` (append-only). Past audit transcripts are at `handover/audits/{CODEX,GEMINI}_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26{,_R2,_R3,_R4,_R5,_R6,...}.md`. Reviewers needing the full chronology read the history doc; reviewers verifying current-state correctness against Phase B prerequisites read THIS packet.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:9990:67:- **Statistical framing** (post-A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 wording corrections): `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows; `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:10003:138:(All round-1..round-6 questions and their closures are recorded in `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md`. The questions below are the round-N+1 set — a fresh reviewer pass that does NOT need to re-litigate prior closures.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PAPER1_V2_1_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:44:The important reproducibility improvements are real. The paper now points the raw v2 logs to `handover/evidence/v2/` [paper:340-343](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_v2_DRAFT_SKELETON_2026-04-24.md:340), and on-disk inspection at this commit confirms exactly 12 files there, matching the expected `(4 seeds × 3 conditions)` bundle. The aggregate JSON also shows zero measurement errors and BUILD_SHA `29ab43a` across runs [results JSON:15-209,408-411](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/E1v2_RESULTS_2026-04-24.json:15).
handover/audits/CODEX_PAPER1_V2_1_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:46:Residual gap: artifact stabilization is not fully finished. The paper still says “final tag pending” and “to be cut” for `paper1-v2.1` [paper:298-299,348](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_v2_DRAFT_SKELETON_2026-04-24.md:298), and `git tag --points-at d349a86` is empty as of 2026-04-25. Appendix C still uses a `.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/...` jsonl path [paper:418](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_v2_DRAFT_SKELETON_2026-04-24.md:418), which undercuts the “stable paths” story. Also, the paper says per-problem proof artifacts “live at `handover/evidence/v2/<problem>_s<seed>_B.lean`” and that proof `.lean` files are archived there [paper:413,426](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_v2_DRAFT_SKELETON_2026-04-24.md:413), but `handover/evidence/v2/` at this commit contains only the 12 `.jsonl` files. That is a real reproducibility overstatement, but I would still score it P1 rather than P0 because the primary raw logs are now in-repo and the paper no longer depends on Docker for minimal reproduction.
handover/audits/CODEX_PAPER1_V2_1_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:78:Residual issue: the “final commit/tag” part is not done yet. The paper still says the final tag is pending/to be cut [paper:298-299,348](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_v2_DRAFT_SKELETON_2026-04-24.md:298), and Appendix C still references `.claude/worktrees/...` [paper:418](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_v2_DRAFT_SKELETON_2026-04-24.md:418). I would not block submission on this alone, but the reproducibility appendix should be cleaned before treating the artifact bundle as final.
handover/audits/CODEX_PAPER1_V2_1_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:91:3. **New P1 REPRO mismatch**: Appendix C still contains a `.claude/worktrees/...` extraction path and claims proof `.lean` files are in `handover/evidence/v2/` [paper:413-426](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_v2_DRAFT_SKELETON_2026-04-24.md:413), but that directory at this commit contains only the 12 raw `.jsonl` files. The paper should either move the proof artifacts there or correct the location claim.

handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:11:**Document split**: this packet is the **stable current-state snapshot** of Phase A at exit — it describes WHAT IS, not how it got here. The chronological history of audit rounds + their in-cycle fix bundles lives in the **companion** document `handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md` (append-only). Past audit transcripts are at `handover/audits/{CODEX,GEMINI}_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26{,_R2,_R3,_R4,_R5,_R6,_R7,...}.md`. Reviewers needing closure-of-prior-finding context read the history doc; reviewers verifying current-state correctness against Phase B prerequisites read THIS packet.

The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:189:- New `parse_swarm_condition_n` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` discriminates `n<digits>` from `oneshot` / `hybrid_v1` / malformed. PREREG_AMENDMENT § 3 condition 2 cleared.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:220:- `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change: per-provider multi-key round-robin. PROVIDERS map now holds a list of env names per provider; `get_client_round_robin` distributes via `_rr_counters` mod `len(clients)`. `/stats` exposes `per_key_requests` for observability. New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:250:- (Q1.a) Each of A1–A7 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the commit message, per memory `feedback_fc_first_problem_handling`. Verify by re-reading each commit message. Are any commits missing FC-trace, or claiming an FC node that doesn't match the actual code change?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:338:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:392:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:402:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5102:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5105:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5106:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5107:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5108:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5109:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5150:| `parse_swarm_condition_n` (A2) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | FC2-N16 InitAI orchestration entry — discriminates `oneshot` vs `n<N>` swarm code paths; FC1-N11 ∏p reached only via swarm | ✅ |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5158:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5416:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5421:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:26:- Cumulative tests: **264 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed** (was 261; +3 run_id unit tests).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5423:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:42:- Codex R2: **CHALLENGE / high** — 3 findings (F2 not recurring; PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 still says "strictest plausible bar" contradicting § 2; A8 packet + TRACE_MATRIX stale on counts + risk #5 + run_corr_id symbol row + "in CI" claim).

Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5626:    15	- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5884:   144	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

Any other anchor-count mismatches? Any other contradictions between round-1 questions and round-2/3 closures?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:6381:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7661:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/SYNTHESIS_2026-04-22.md:79:| `bus.rs:416-424` `Box<dyn>` downcast ~5× 加速 | 不是 per-tx，仅 bus-side invest/refund/settle 路径 | ~1.05-1.1× 加速 | **Claude 严重失真**（5× vs 1.1×） |
handover/audits/SYNTHESIS_2026-04-22.md:137:| 外部 agent 接入 | 日增 agent 数 + 活跃外部 agent 占比 | 连续 7 天 0 接入 / 活跃外部 <10% |
handover/audits/SYNTHESIS_2026-04-22.md:138:| 解题效率 | 日均解题数 + 解决率 | 日均 <1 / 解决率 <50% |
handover/audits/SYNTHESIS_2026-04-22.md:139:| 经济健康度 | Coin 流通深度 + Gini 系数 | Gini >0.8（极度不平等）/ 流通深度 <0.1（僵化） |
handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_2026-04-21.md:83:| `bus.rs:416-424` `Box<dyn>` downcast | ~5× 加速 | **~1.05× - 1.1×** 加速 | Claude 的估计严重失真。`downcast` 本质上是一次 vtable 查找加一次类型 ID 比较，耗时仅为几个 CPU cycle。在只有 3-5 个 tool 的情况下，`enum` dispatch（通常是 jump table）相比动态分派的优势微乎其微。5倍的加速意味着 downcast 占了函数 80% 的执行时间，这在物理上是不可能的。实际加速效果几乎无法测量。 |
handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_2026-04-21.md:106:    -   由于 `γ(n)` 是一个 S 型增长函数（在初期是凸函数，后期是凹函数），将解题数分散到多个账户会使每个账户都停留在曲线的低收益起始段。因此，`Profit(M, N) < Profit(1, N)`。

handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:285:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:289:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5051:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5054:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5055:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5056:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation in CI without a live API. **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5097:| `parse_swarm_condition_n` (A2) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | FC2-N16 InitAI orchestration entry — discriminates `oneshot` vs `n<N>` swarm code paths; FC1-N11 ∏p reached only via swarm | ✅ |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5141:7. ~~**Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate** — Plan agent's recommendation in N-experiments brainstorm. Phase A6 deferred; will land before Phase B (homogeneous experiments).~~ **A6 LANDED** (commit pending): `fc_trace.rs` module + 6 wired anchor sites. Implementation chose pure stdlib over the `tracing` crate to avoid a new dep tree; the macro surface (`emit_event` + `FcId` enum) was kept small so Phase D+ can swap to a real `tracing-subscriber` bridge locally.
