handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5738:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5055:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5739:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5056:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation in CI without a live API. **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5740:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5064:A0b added the missing `tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs` (was only in `.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/`). 17 ✅ rows in TRACE_MATRIX now have automated witness tests. Symbol drift is now caught at `cargo test` time, not at next dual audit.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7104:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:12:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7105:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation in CI without a live API. **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7115:handover/alignment/OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT_2026-04-25.md:52:If/when Phase 11+ lands an in-process re-init mechanism (TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N41), it can use `Result<(), TrustRootError>` from `verify_trust_root` instead of letting the panic propagate. At that point the panic becomes the *uncaught* path and structured retry becomes the *caught* path. Constitution still does not need to change — both paths land at FC3-E14.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7187:handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3:**Session Summary**: B7 (Trust Root + Boot freeze) → 用户 atomic-alignment critique (3 flowcharts) → B7 alignment fix → B7-extra rollback toggle + calibration scripts → dual audit round-1 VETO/VETO → 13-fix landing → simplifier pass → **constitution amendment (sudo)**: V.1.1 sudo scope + V.1.2 ArchitectAI commit authority + V.1.3 JudgeAI→Veto-AI + V.3 amendment log → re-audit round 2 VETO/PASS (Codex caught self-inflicted regression: Q7.b silently absorbed TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panics) → round-2 fix → re-audit round 3 CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE (problem_file_missing absorption + boot preflight `||true` exit-discard + EXIT=0+empty PPUT_RESULT non-exhaustive) → round-3 fix (commit `d0d474e`) → **round-4 audit in flight**. **187/187 cargo test PASS** + 20 ignored. Trust Root manifest **20 files** (was 15 — added main.rs / Cargo.lock / runner.sh / compute_p0.py per audit). User authorized auto-research overnight to PROCEED on PASS/PASS.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7304:experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs:3:// Codex#2 + Gemini Q4 (A8 round-1 audit): `run_corr_id` was generated
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7317:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7458:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9232:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9238:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:95:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9296:handover/preregistration/README.md:41:  seeds: <e.g. 4 Boltzmann seeds: 141421, 31415, 2718, 2357>
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9334:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:8:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9335:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9336:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit (swarm), FC2-N20 mr tick (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic (swarm), FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (swarm, with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (verify_omega_detailed × 2 + verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9337:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:12:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (3 entries: `llm_proxy.py` + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9338:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e5 (post-A8e4, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-5 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — same split as round-4): five narrow doc/source-text fixes; zero source-code changes. **J1** Round-4 outcome + A8e4 fixes shipped section added to the packet (was missing — Codex R5#1 caught the gap). **J2** `<pending>` commit placeholders for A8e2/A8e3/A8e4 replaced with actual SHAs (`0af47b7` / `3d38ba5` / `8693789`); A8e5 self-reference now uses "this commit" to break the placeholder-staleness pattern. **J3** packet § 6 Q6 question text reworded: "24 → 30 / 6 new entries" → "24 → 34 / 10 new entries"; full enumerated list added. **J4** TRACE_MATRIX § 1 top-bullet TR-deltas corrected: A5 `25 → 26` → `26 → 27`; A6 `26 → 27` → `27 → 28` (matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header). **J5** round-3 retrospective text about `make_pput` arg count: 21 → 24 (Gemini R5 spot-check; F1's `run_id` parameter brought the post-A8e count from 21 to 24). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (no new files; doc-only changes). All findings are documentary; both auditors confirmed the code is sound + ready for Phase B.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9339:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9340:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9341:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9342:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9386:handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:14:| FC1-N5 | `rtool` | `ReadTool::project`<br>`DefaultReadTool::project`<br>`TuringBus::snapshot` | `src/sdk/read_tool.rs:24`<br>`src/sdk/read_tool.rs:37`<br>`src/bus.rs:802` | `Y`<br>`N`<br>`Y` | `medium` | Phase Z added an explicit `rtool` trait, but the current evaluator still reads state by calling `bus.snapshot()` directly. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9387:handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:16:| FC1-N7 | `δ / AI` | `ResilientLLMClient::generate`<br>`match client.generate(&request).await` | `src/drivers/llm_http.rs:84`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:211`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:644` | `Y`<br>`N`<br>`N` | `high` | These are the actual LLM call surfaces that map prompt input to model output in oneshot and swarm runs. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9388:handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:21:| FC1-N12 | `individual p predicates` | `ForbiddenPatternPredicate`<br>`SorryPredicate`<br>`PayloadSizePredicate`<br>`Lean4Oracle::verify_partial`<br>`Lean4Oracle::verify_omega_detailed` | `src/sdk/predicate.rs:106`<br>`src/sdk/predicate.rs:124`<br>`src/sdk/predicate.rs:139`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/lean4_oracle.rs:248`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/lean4_oracle.rs:183` | `Y`<br>`Y`<br>`Y`<br>`Y`<br>`Y` | `medium` | Concrete predicate logic exists, but it is split between Phase Z predicate structs and the still-separate Lean oracle verifier. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9389:handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:22:| FC1-N13 | `wtool` | `WriteTool::write`<br>`DefaultWriteTool::write`<br>`TuringBus::append`<br>`TuringBus::append_oracle_accepted` | `src/sdk/write_tool.rs:29`<br>`src/sdk/write_tool.rs:84`<br>`src/bus.rs:324`<br>`src/bus.rs:347` | `Y`<br>`N`<br>`Y`<br>`Y` | `medium` | The explicit write-tool contract exists, but the current harness usually enters the write path by calling bus append methods directly. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9390:handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:23:| FC1-N14 | `Q_{t+1}` on successful write | `TuringBus::append_internal`<br>`Kernel::append`<br>`TuringBus::halt_with_reason` | `src/bus.rs:421`<br>`src/kernel.rs:108`<br>`src/bus.rs:207` | `N`<br>`Y`<br>`Y` | `medium` | Successful writes update tape and ledger in `append_internal`, and terminal writes additionally advance `q_state` through `halt_with_reason`. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9391:handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:30:| FC2-N21 | `initAI --once→ Q0` | `Kernel::new`<br>`TuringBus::new`<br>`TuringBus::init` | `src/kernel.rs:50`<br>`src/bus.rs:115`<br>`src/bus.rs:299` | `N`<br>`N`<br>`Y` | `high` | Kernel construction, bus construction, and `init()` collectively materialize the initial live machine state before iteration begins. |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9392:handover/alignment/CODE_CANDIDATES_2026-04-22.md:33:| FC2-N24 | `clock` | `TuringBus::clock`<br>`for tx in 0..max_transactions`<br>`let tick_interval: usize` | `src/bus.rs:66`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:485`<br>`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:459` | `N`<br>`N`<br>`N` | `high` | Scheduling comes from the transaction loop and tick interval, while the bus tracks a monotone committed clock counter. |

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9840:   179	- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9845:   184	- New `parse_swarm_condition_n` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` discriminates `n<digits>` from `oneshot` / `hybrid_v1` / malformed. PREREG_AMENDMENT § 3 condition 2 cleared.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9868:   207	- New module `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs`. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10020:    58	| `parse_swarm_condition_n` (A2) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | FC2-N16 InitAI orchestration entry — discriminates `oneshot` vs `n<N>` swarm code paths; FC1-N11 ∏p reached only via swarm | ✅ |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10028:    66	| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10290:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R3.md:7528:   The code fix is real: one `run_id` is minted and threaded through FC events and `make_pput` (`experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs:17-30`, `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:495-499`, `:1507-1510`, `:1573-1576`), and swarm FC1-N12 emits exist at all three verify sites (`evaluator.rs:1017-1024`, `:1041-1048`, `:1318-1325`). But the A8 packet still states only oneshot FC1-N12 coverage (`handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:147`, `:199`) and still describes the pre-F1 `run_corr_id`/`make_pput` millisecond drift as current (`:202`). TRACE_MATRIX also still has “commit pending” and “6 wired anchor sites” in open-work text (`handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:100`). This does not invalidate the implementation, but it fails the “auditable as a unit” packet standard.

Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10316:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10317:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10322:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:42:- Codex R2: **CHALLENGE / high** — 3 findings (F2 not recurring; PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 still says "strictest plausible bar" contradicting § 2; A8 packet + TRACE_MATRIX stale on counts + risk #5 + run_corr_id symbol row + "in CI" claim).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10323:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:49:- **G2** (Codex R2#2): `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded to remove the residual "strictest plausible bar is conservative" phrase that contradicted § 2's wording correction. Re-hashed in Trust Root.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10330:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:80:- (RQ10) Verify H1: re-grep `A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md` for any remaining "conservative" / "strictest" claim about `p_0`. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10331:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:83:- (RQ13) Verify packet self-consistency: any other "conservative" claims about the substitution? Any other anchor-count mismatches? Any other contradictions between round-1 questions and round-2/3 closures?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10332:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:179:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:4644:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:4647:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:4648:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 27 → 30 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:4689:| `parse_swarm_condition_n` (A2) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | FC2-N16 InitAI orchestration entry — discriminates `oneshot` vs `n<N>` swarm code paths; FC1-N11 ∏p reached only via swarm | ✅ |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:4733:7. ~~**Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate** — Plan agent's recommendation in N-experiments brainstorm. Phase A6 deferred; will land before Phase B (homogeneous experiments).~~ **A6 LANDED** (commit pending): `fc_trace.rs` module + 6 wired anchor sites. Implementation chose pure stdlib over the `tracing` crate to avoid a new dep tree; the macro surface (`emit_event` + `FcId` enum) was kept small so Phase D+ can swap to a real `tracing-subscriber` bridge locally.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9203:     8	- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9206:    11	- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9207:    12	- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 27 → 30 (proxy + 2 smoke scripts).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9865:handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:52:    **CHALLENGE**. The battery is inconsistent. While some tests like `fc1_n4_tape_constructible_with_time_arrow` are strong witnesses that call methods, others like `fc1_n1_q_state_carrier_present` use `std::any::type_name::<T>()`. This only proves the type *exists*, not that its constitutionally relevant API is intact. This is a weak form of witnessing that could allow for silent drift if, for example, a key method on `TuringBus` were removed but the type itself remained. The weaker witnesses should be strengthened to call the specific functions or methods cited as evidence in `TRACE_MATRIX_v2`.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:10126:    60	- (Q2.a) Are the 17 witnesses TRULY witnesses (i.e., would fail to compile if the symbol is removed/renamed) or are some only "type_name::<T>()" calls that wouldn't catch behavioral regressions?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:10177:   111	  - "F-2026-04-25-05"  # B7-extra round-1 dual VETO including Trust Root manifest gaps
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:10201:   135	axiom: "Every pub fn / struct / enum / trait / const in src/ must carry /// TRACE_MATRIX FCx-Nx: <role> doc-comment OR be filed as orphan with explicit cases/Cxxx or PREREG-§n.m justification in TRACE_MATRIX_v?.md"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:10208:   142	message: "REMINDER (R-015 / Alignment Standard): edit touches a pub symbol in a Rust file. New OR modified pub items MUST carry `/// TRACE_MATRIX FC?-N?: <role>` doc-comment AND have an entry in handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v?.md (current: v1_2026-04-25). If this is genuinely orphan, file under TRACE_MATRIX § 3 with explicit Constitutional Justification (cases/Cxxx or PREREG-§n.m). Untraced pub symbols cause silent constitutional drift."
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:10282:    52	    **CHALLENGE**. The battery is inconsistent. While some tests like `fc1_n4_tape_constructible_with_time_arrow` are strong witnesses that call methods, others like `fc1_n1_q_state_carrier_present` use `std::any::type_name::<T>()`. This only proves the type *exists*, not that its constitutionally relevant API is intact. This is a weak form of witnessing that could allow for silent drift if, for example, a key method on `TuringBus` were removed but the type itself remained. The weaker witnesses should be strengthened to call the specific functions or methods cited as evidence in `TRACE_MATRIX_v2`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:10342:For Gate H, `j-RR` passes when `RR_j <= p_0`; the frozen PREREG says this is a descriptive point check, not an inferential test, and `p_0` is normally calibrated before use ([PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:420), [line 432](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:432)). The amendment substitutes the ceiling `0.10` and calls it the “strictest possible substitute” ([PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:23), [line 25](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:25)). That is backwards for a `<=` guardrail: `0.10` is the least strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. It does not inflate Type-I error because `j-RR` is outside the inferential family ([PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:414)), but it can weaken the regression guardrail versus any eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:10372:For Gate H, `j-RR` passes when `RR_j <= p_0`; the frozen PREREG says this is a descriptive point check, not an inferential test, and `p_0` is normally calibrated before use ([PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:420), [line 432](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:432)). The amendment substitutes the ceiling `0.10` and calls it the “strictest possible substitute” ([PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:23), [line 25](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:25)). That is backwards for a `<=` guardrail: `0.10` is the least strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. It does not inflate Type-I error because `j-RR` is outside the inferential family ([PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:414)), but it can weaken the regression guardrail versus any eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`.

handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:222:1. (**Codex R8#1 — substantive**) PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8's audit-requirements text still contains the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Logical contradiction with the post-M4 § 2 + § 3 framing.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:223:2. (Codex R8#2) This history doc's round-7 entry left an unresolved placeholder `<aaedc9d-successor>` for the A8e8 commit SHA; cumulative table row 7 still says "pending pending pending" despite round-7 verdicts being recorded above.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:224:3. (Codex R8#3) Audit runner scripts default `A8_AUDIT_ROUND=R2` (oldest fallback) and emit pre-A8e header metadata ("261 PASS / 30-entry manifest") — re-running the runners regenerates stale audit artifacts.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:226:**Round-8 fixes shipped (`A8e9`, this commit)** — three fixes:
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:228:- **N2** (Codex R8#2): Round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e`; cumulative table row 7 sealed with the actual round-7 verdicts (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE) and finding count (4 Codex + 2 Gemini, including 1 substantive PREREG logic finding); round-8 row added with this round's verdicts.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:229:- **N3** (Codex R8#3): Runner scripts default `A8_AUDIT_ROUND` updated; header metadata refreshed to current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest).
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:245:| 9 | pending | pending | pending | — | — | ~5 |
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:247:Cumulative cost so far ~$45 (8 rounds × ~$5–7); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 9 substantive findings caught + closed across 8 rounds** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 = 9 real bugs discovered + fixed pre-Phase B). The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency.
handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:78:### 2. `src/bus.rs:416-424` `Box<dyn TuringTool>` + downcast claim
handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:80:Again, the performance concern is overstated as phrased. `debit_wallet` and `credit_wallet` do perform repeated `manifest()` + `downcast_mut::<WalletTool>()` scans (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:416-437`), but those functions are not on every transaction. They are used on bus-side invest/refund/hayek/settlement paths (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:233,240,346,359,383-412`), while evaluator-side invest does its own separate wallet downcasts (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:694-716`). The deeper issue is architectural duplication and bus bypass, not the raw dynamic-dispatch cost alone.
handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_2026-04-22.md:84:This one is directionally real. `trace_ancestors` allocates both a `Vec` and a `HashSet<String>` every call (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/ledger.rs:115-135`). It is then called from `lineage_score` and `is_frontier` inside Boltzmann routing (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/sdk/actor.rs:47-74,77-95`), which means append-parent selection can repeat that work per frontier node. However, it is not a universal runtime hot path either: the Phase 7 batch log shows only `step`/`omega_wtool` traffic and no `append` usage at all (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/logs/templadder_n8_20260421T164014.jsonl:1-9`).

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5807:./handover/ai-direct/AUTO_RESEARCH_NOTEPAD.md:72:**TL;DR**: when a Q7.b "synthetic UNSOLVED on any non-zero exit" was added in round-1 fix to address sampling bias, it silently absorbed TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panics into "valid" calibration data — neutralizing the B1 fix that the same round was supposed to deliver. **Codex caught it in round-2; Gemini missed it (PASS).** Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" + memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`, conservative reading wins → VETO. Round-2 fix (commit `1df1f62`) discriminates exit codes: only timeout (124) emits synthetic row; any other crash ABORT BATCH with grep for TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED. Round-3 Gemini returned CHALLENGE on a follow-up exhaustiveness gap (EXIT=0 + empty PPUT_RESULT case fell through to generic crash branch); fixed in same notepad-update cycle. **Lesson**: when fixing a sampling-bias bug, the fix itself can become a security bypass; always re-audit fixes before promoting to PASS. The dual-audit's value is exactly in this kind of cross-checking.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5812:./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3:**Session Summary**: B7 (Trust Root + Boot freeze) → 用户 atomic-alignment critique (3 flowcharts) → B7 alignment fix → B7-extra rollback toggle + calibration scripts → dual audit round-1 VETO/VETO → 13-fix landing → simplifier pass → **constitution amendment (sudo)**: V.1.1 sudo scope + V.1.2 ArchitectAI commit authority + V.1.3 JudgeAI→Veto-AI + V.3 amendment log → re-audit round 2 VETO/PASS (Codex caught self-inflicted regression: Q7.b silently absorbed TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panics) → round-2 fix → re-audit round 3 CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE (problem_file_missing absorption + boot preflight `||true` exit-discard + EXIT=0+empty PPUT_RESULT non-exhaustive) → round-3 fix (commit `d0d474e`) → **round-4 audit in flight**. **187/187 cargo test PASS** + 20 ignored. Trust Root manifest **20 files** (was 15 — added main.rs / Cargo.lock / runner.sh / compute_p0.py per audit). User authorized auto-research overnight to PROCEED on PASS/PASS.

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:4768:Now give your INDEPENDENT round-1 audit. Be specific: cite §/paragraph for every finding. Do not speculate beyond the documents.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:4830:- **§ 9.1 (Family Timing):** The introduction of a pre-committed `k_max = 10` to define a frozen `N_max = 44` for all multiplicity corrections is the standard, correct way to handle a data-dependent family size in a pre-registration. It resolves the timing paradox cleanly. The choice to accept a potential loss of power if `k < k_max` is the right trade-off for maintaining statistical validity.

handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R3.md:5:**Test baseline**: 264 PASS + 29 ignored + 0 failed (Rust); 15/15 PASS (Python proxy tests)
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R3.md:14:The team has demonstrated diligence in addressing the round-1 VETO and round-2 CHALLENGE findings. The G1 fix, in particular, provides a robust, recurring conformance gate for the Python proxy's round-robin logic, satisfying the core requirement of the original VETO. The G2 and G3 fixes correctly address the documentary and procedural gaps identified in round 2.
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R3.md:29:    This describes the millisecond-drift problem that was a key finding in the round-1 audit (Codex#2, Gemini Q4). The packet's own summary of round-1 fixes correctly states that fix **F1** closed this:
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R3.md:44:-   **(G1) Python Conformance Test Integration**: **VERIFIED**. The new Rust test `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` correctly shells out to the Python test suite. It is present in the Trust Root manifest (`genesis_payload.toml`) and the immutability test (`trust_root_immutability.rs:130`). This successfully elevates the Python test from a manual check to a recurring, automated conformance gate, satisfying the round-1 VETO.

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:615:If realized k < k_max: we are conservative (lose power) — accepted tradeoff for prereg discipline. Spare family slots (positions k+1 .. k_max in Holm sort order) are simply not used; their α budget is forfeit.

handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND3_2026-04-26.md:16:- **§ 9.1 (Family Timing):** The introduction of a pre-committed `k_max = 10` to define a frozen `N_max = 44` for all multiplicity corrections is the standard, correct way to handle a data-dependent family size in a pre-registration. It resolves the timing paradox cleanly. The choice to accept a potential loss of power if `k < k_max` is the right trade-off for maintaining statistical validity.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:464:Cumulative cost so far ~$45 (8 rounds × ~$5–7); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 9 substantive findings caught + closed across 8 rounds** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 = 9 real bugs discovered + fixed pre-Phase B). The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:501:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:505:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:506:- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:559:"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:569:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5425:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5427:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5428:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5429:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (3 entries: `llm_proxy.py` + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5430:- A8e9 (post-A8e8, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-8 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): three fixes including one substantive PREREG logic miss. **N1 (substantive Codex R8#1)**: PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 audit-requirements text still contained the stale claim "Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first" — round-7 M4 fixed § 2(b)'s identical false claim but missed the parallel text in § 8. Now reworded so § 2 + § 3 + § 8 all consistently express "substitution operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; § 3 conditions are pre-requisites for calibration to run, not guarantees of completion before any phase". Re-hashed in Trust Root. **N2 (Codex R8#2)**: A8_AUDIT_HISTORY round-7 entry stamped with actual A8e8 SHA `857872e` (was `<aaedc9d-successor>` placeholder); cumulative table row 7 sealed (CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE; 6 findings; 1 substantive); row 8 added (CHALLENGE/PASS; 3 findings; 1 substantive); row 9 left as pending. **N3 (Codex R8#3)**: audit runner script header metadata refreshed — was emitting "264 PASS + 30-entry manifest" (round-1 era values) every time the runners executed; now emits current state (265 PASS / 35-entry manifest) and references the history doc for chronology. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35. **Real-bug yield through 8 rounds: 9 substantive findings caught + closed** (5 routing/correctness in R1, 1 fail-closed gate in R3, 1 routing collision in R6, 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7, 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5431:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5433:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5434:- A8e5 (post-A8e4, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-5 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — same split as round-4): five narrow doc/source-text fixes; zero source-code changes. **J1** Round-4 outcome + A8e4 fixes shipped section added to the packet (was missing — Codex R5#1 caught the gap). **J2** `<pending>` commit placeholders for A8e2/A8e3/A8e4 replaced with actual SHAs (`0af47b7` / `3d38ba5` / `8693789`); A8e5 self-reference now uses "this commit" to break the placeholder-staleness pattern. **J3** packet § 6 Q6 question text reworded: "24 → 30 / 6 new entries" → "24 → 34 / 10 new entries"; full enumerated list added. **J4** TRACE_MATRIX § 1 top-bullet TR-deltas corrected: A5 `25 → 26` → `26 → 27`; A6 `26 → 27` → `27 → 28` (matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header). **J5** round-3 retrospective text about `make_pput` arg count: 21 → 24 (Gemini R5 spot-check; F1's `run_id` parameter brought the post-A8e count from 21 to 24). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (no new files; doc-only changes). All findings are documentary; both auditors confirmed the code is sound + ready for Phase B.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5435:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5436:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5437:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5438:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:6222:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:6226:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:6227:    32	- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the substitution is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** — see § 8 for the operative rule. If § 3 conditions complete before Phase E, calibration UPGRADES the bar; if not, Phase E proceeds with the ceiling substitution. There is no path under this amendment where Phase E is blocked by absence of calibration — that was an earlier draft's misclaim, removed by A8e8 fix M4 after Codex round-7 audit.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:6280:    85	"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:6290:    95	- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)

handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:52:    **CHALLENGE**. The battery is inconsistent. While some tests like `fc1_n4_tape_constructible_with_time_arrow` are strong witnesses that call methods, others like `fc1_n1_q_state_carrier_present` use `std::any::type_name::<T>()`. This only proves the type *exists*, not that its constitutionally relevant API is intact. This is a weak form of witnessing that could allow for silent drift if, for example, a key method on `TuringBus` were removed but the type itself remained. The weaker witnesses should be strengthened to call the specific functions or methods cited as evidence in `TRACE_MATRIX_v2`.

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3781:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.

handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8A_2026-04-22.md:41:    - `find_map` 正确地遍历 `Vec<Box<dyn TuringTool>>`，并安全地尝试将每个 tool 向下转型为 `WalletTool`。
handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8A_2026-04-22.md:47:    - 结论：性能开销是为实现宪法规定的“快照隔离”而付出的合理代价。在当前实验规模（N<100 agents）下，这种开销是可见的但并非瓶颈。如果未来系统扩展到数千个 agent，此处的性能可能需要重新评估（例如使用 `im::HashMap` 等持久化数据结构），但对于当前修复而言，`.clone()` 是正确选择。
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:27:**This round**: the author claims to have applied all 10 round-1 fixes. The PREREG has a `## changelog` section listing each fix with cross-references to the auditor that flagged it.

The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.
